originalTitle: Utah boarding school where Paris Hilton alleged abuse as a teen loses its licence
summary: Hilton said the facility "failed the children", as regulators cited neglect and use of unnecessary restraints.
content:
A girls' boarding school where US socialite Paris Hilton alleged she was abused as a teenager has had its licence revoked.

Utah state regulators cited unnecessary restraint, aggressive physical contact and instances of neglect among noncompliance issues at Provo Canyon School's Springville campus going back to 2025.

Hilton, who has campaigned to close the school and testified before Congress about her experience there in the 1990s, said the facility "failed the children in its care".

The school said it was considering an appeal against the decision. The girls' facility is described on its website as an intensive, psychiatric youth residential treatment centre for girls 12 to 18.

The Utah Department of Health and Human services (DHHS) said the school had "failed to provide applicable health and safety services for clients".

Its residential treatment licence has been cancelled and all services at the campus must be terminated by 6 August, said the letter , external from state regulators.

The school has 15 days to challenge the decision.

Social media personality Hilton, 45, said in a statement to the BBC: "For more than fifty years, children came forward with stories of abuse, neglect, and trauma.

"Today, the state confirmed what survivors have known all along: Provo Canyon School failed the children in its care. I was one of those children.

"I know what it feels like to cry for help and believe no-one is coming. Today, children still inside that facility know someone is finally coming to protect them."

Watch: Paris Hilton recounts childhood abuse to US Congress

She added that no institution was too powerful to be held accountable: "When survivors refuse to stay silent, change is possible."

School officials told local media they were "evaluating all available legal and administrative options, including an appeal".

"As this is an ongoing matter, we are limited in what we can say at this time. Our priority remains providing safe, high-quality care and support for adolescents and their families, and we remain committed to serving those in need."

The school's programme has faced closure demands from former residents besides Hilton, and complaints were lodged to the state's licensing agency last month.

Health officials have also imposed temporary restrictions on the school's boys' campus in Provo, Utah, in June.

The boys campus "continues to serve those under our care, but is not permitted to accept new admissions at this time", its website notes.

An inquiry found that staff had failed to protect a boy during a fight and did not seek immediate medical care for his injuries.

Hotel heiress Paris Hilton outside the US Capitol on 17 December 2024.

Hilton spent almost a year at the Springville, Utah, school in the 1990s, when the school was under different ownership. She spoke out about her experience during a documentary about her life released in 2020.

She alleged that she was physically assaulted, made to take medication and placed in solitary confinement in programmes aimed at reforming her behaviour.

"My parents were promised that tough love would fix me and that sending me across the country was the only way," she said in 2021, adding that her experience at the facilities "haunts her to this day".

The hotel heiress, who has advocated against the "troubled teen industry" , testified in front of Congress in 2024 and across state legislatures to enact laws to protect teens from a network of private, for-profit centres for children with perceived behavioural issues.

Troubled US teens left traumatised by tough love camps

Paris Hilton pushes for US youth care abuse reform
chineseTitle: 帕丽斯·希尔顿 (Paris Hilton) 因虐待犹他州寄宿学校青少年而被吊销执照
chineseSummary: 希尔顿表示，该设施“辜负了孩子们的期望”，监管机构称其疏忽和使用了不必要的限制。
chineseContent:
美国名媛帕丽斯·希尔顿 (Paris Hilton) 声称自己在青少年时期曾遭受虐待，一所女子寄宿学校已被吊销执照。

犹他州监管机构指出，自 2025 年起，普罗沃峡谷学校斯普林维尔校区就存在不必要的限制、侵略性身体接触和忽视等违规行为。

希尔顿曾发起关闭学校的运动，并在国会作证，讲述了她在 20 世纪 90 年代在那里的经历，她表示，该设施“辜负了其照顾的孩子们”。

学校表示正在考虑对该决定提出上诉。该女童设施在其网站上被描述为一个针对 12 至 18 岁女童的强化、精神病学青少年寄宿治疗中心。

犹他州卫生与公众服务部 (DHHS) 表示，学校“未能为客户提供适用的健康和安全服务”。

这封来自州监管机构的信函称，其住院治疗许可证已被取消，校园内的所有服务必须在 8 月 6 日之前终止。

学校有 15 天的时间对该决定提出质疑。

45 岁的社交媒体名人希尔顿在给 BBC 的一份声明中表示：“五十多年来，孩子们纷纷站出来讲述虐待、忽视和创伤的故事。

“今天，该州证实了幸存者一直以来都知道的事情：普罗沃峡谷学校未能照顾到这些孩子。我就是那些孩子之一。

“我知道哭泣求救并相信没有人会来是什么感觉。今天，仍在该设施内的孩子们知道有人终于会来保护他们。”

观看：帕丽斯·希尔顿向美国国会讲述童年虐待经历

她补充说，没有哪个机构强大到可以承担责任：“当幸存者拒绝保持沉默时，改变就有可能。”

学校官员告诉当地媒体，他们正在“评估所有可用的法律和行政选项，包括上诉”。

“由于这是一个持续的问题，我们目前能说的有限。我们的首要任务仍然是为青少年及其家人提供安全、高质量的护理和支持，我们仍然致力于为有需要的人提供服务。”

除了希尔顿之外，该学校的项目还面临着前居民的关闭要求，上个月已向该州许可机构提出投诉。

六月，卫生官员还对犹他州普罗沃市学校的男校校园实施了临时限制。

其网站指出，男子校园“继续为我们照顾的学生提供服务，但目前不允许接受新的入学”。

一项调查发现，工作人员在打架期间未能保护一名男孩，也没有立即就其受伤寻求医疗护理。

2024 年 12 月 17 日，酒店女继承人帕丽斯·希尔顿 (Paris Hilton) 在美国国会大厦外。

20 世纪 90 年代，希尔顿在犹他州斯普林维尔的学校待了将近一年，当时该学校属于不同的所有权。她在 2020 年发布的一部关于她生活的纪录片中讲述了自己的经历。

她声称，在旨在改变她行为的项目中，她遭到人身攻击、被迫服药并被单独监禁。

她在 2021 年表示，“他们向我父母保证，严酷的爱会治愈我，送我到全国各地是唯一的办法”，并补充说，她在这些设施的经历“至今仍困扰着她”。

这位酒店女继承人主张反对“陷入困境的青少年行业”，她于 2024 年在国会和各州立法机构面前作证，要求制定法律，保护青少年免受私人营利性儿童行为问题中心网络的影响。

陷入困境的美国青少年因艰难的爱情营而受到创伤

帕丽斯·希尔顿推动美国青少年护理虐待改革
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
